headline: settings-integrity no longer warns forever on .claude/settings.local.json drift

bullets: settings-integrity: when the SessionStart drift warning fires for .claude/settings.local.json (gitignored, untracked), it now warns once and rebaselines silently. Previously the stored hash was never updated for untracked files, so a single drift would warn every session forever — there's no commit-or-restore recovery path for a gitignored file. Tracked files (.claude/settings.json) keep ratcheting until you commit or restore.
